{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.57222 | longitude = 139.85972 | deities = War dead, including 55,361 spirits from Tochigi Prefecture | founded = 1872 (Meiji 5) | structure = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = April 28 | map = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps display |{{location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Display Tochigi Prefecture map }} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|12=draft}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[Boshin War]], [[First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|12=draft}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|12=draft}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Utsunomiya]] [[Category:War memorials in Japan]] [[Category:Meiji period establishments]] [[Category:Pacific War memorials]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] }} [[en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]] {{citation needed|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.57222 | longitude = 139.85972 | deities = War dead, including 55,361 spirits from Tochigi Prefecture | founded = 1872 (Meiji 5) | structure = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = April 28 | map = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps display |{{location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Display Tochigi Prefecture map }} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|12=draft}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[Boshin War]], [[First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|12=draft}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|12=draft}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Utsunomiya]] [[Category:War memorials in Japan]] [[Category:Meiji period establishments]] [[Category:Pacific War memorials]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] }} [[en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]] {{citation needed|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.57222 | longitude = 139.85972 | deities = War dead, including 55,361 spirits from Tochigi Prefecture | founded = 1872 (Meiji 5) | structure = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = April 28 | map = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps display |{{location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Display Tochigi Prefecture map }} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|12=draft}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[Boshin War]], [[First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|12=draft}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|12=draft}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Utsunomiya]] [[Category:War memorials in Japan]] [[Category:Meiji period establishments]] [[Category:Pacific War memorials]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]] {{citation needed|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.57222 | longitude = 139.85972 | deities = War dead, including 55,361 spirits from Tochigi Prefecture | founded = 1872 (Meiji 5) | structure = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = April 28 | map = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps display |{{location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Display Tochigi Prefecture map }} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[Boshin War]], [[First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|12=draft|12=draft}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|12=draft|12=draft}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Utsunomiya]] [[Category:War memorials in Japan]] [[Category:Meiji period establishments]] [[Category:Pacific War memorials]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]]

{{afc comment|1=see [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.57222 | longitude = 139.85972 | deities = War dead, including 55,361 spirits from Tochigi Prefecture | founded = 1872 (Meiji 5) | structure = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = April 28 | map = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps display |{{location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Display Tochigi Prefecture map }} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[Boshin War]], [[First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Utsunomiya]] [[Category:War memorials in Japan]] [[Category:Meiji period establishments]] [[Category:Pacific War memorials]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]]

revision.comment: 33 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:栃木県護国神社 up to rev 99332893
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 栃木県護国神社（栃木縣護國神社） |画像 = [[ファイル:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]陽西町1-37 |位置 = {{Coord|36|34|20|N|139|51|35|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=栃木県護国神社}} |祭神 = 戦没者 |社格 = 内務大臣指定護国神社 |創建 = [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]） |本殿 = 流造銅板葺 |別名 = |例祭 = [[4月28日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps を表示 |{{Location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|栃木県護国神社}}|position=left}} |栃木県地図を表示 }} }}
'''栃木県護国神社（栃木縣護國神社）'''（とちぎけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）であり、[[境内]]には護国会館がある。
[[明治維新]]、[[日清戦争]]、[[日露戦争]]、[[大東亜戦争]]などの[[戦没者]]や[[警察]]、[[消防]]、[[自衛隊]]の[[殉職]]者などの旧[[宇都宮藩]]、栃木県関係の戦没者55,361柱を祀る。
[[天皇]]・[[皇后]]が日本全国で唯一公式に[[親拝]]している護国神社で、[[世界平和]]と人類共存を祈る神社とされている。大東亜戦争後、[[海外]]各[[戦地]]の[[慰霊]][[巡拝]]を[[国籍]]や当時の敵味方の区別なく永年に亘って継続しており、[[皇族]]や[[神社本庁]]とも所縁が深く、天皇・皇后が親拝した時の写真が[[本殿]]正面左右上方に掲げられている。
なお、本殿手前には、[[船村徹]]が寄贈した[[大日本帝国海軍]]の[[零式艦上戦闘機]]の模型が展示されている。
== 歴史 == [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）、[[戊辰戦争]]に殉じた宇都宮藩[[藩主]][[戸田忠恕]]および[[藩士]]96柱の合計97柱を宇都宮市馬場町字表尾山に祀った'''宇都宮[[招魂社]]'''が起源である<ref name="#1">『栃木県神社誌』，221頁</ref>。以降、栃木県出身の戦没者、殉職[[警察官]]、殉職[[消防士]]らの殉死者を[[合祀]]している。[[昭和]]14年（[[1939年]]）、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]令により[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]指定護国神社となり、'''栃木県護国神社'''と改称し、翌年の昭和15年（[[1940年]]）には現在地に[[遷座]]し、殉職[[自衛官]]の殉死者も合祀している。
昭和22年（[[1947年]]）に'''彰徳神社'''と改称したが占領軍が去った昭和28年（[[1953年]]）に栃木県護国神社と改復称した<ref name="#1"/>。
[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）[[7月25日]]には[[上皇明仁|天皇]]のたっての希望により、天皇・[[上皇后美智子|皇后]]が境内敷地入口にある大鳥居から本殿までを自ら歩き、公式に親拝している。
平成22年（[[2010年]]）[[3月18日]]、シイの巨木の移植が行われた<ref name="shimotsuke1">{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-3-19|page=24}}</ref>。当社が昭和15年に現在地に[[遷座]]した際に境内南側の敷地に植えられたもので、[[栃木県道70号宇都宮今市線|県道70号線]]の拡張工事のために伐採される予定であったが、保存を望む声が高まり大鳥居の左側に移植されることになった<ref name="shimotsuke1"/>。
平成22年[[4月11日]]、第39回元満州開拓青年義勇隊慰霊祭が境内の慰霊碑前で行われた<ref name="shimotsuke2">{{cite news|title=宇都宮・元満州開拓義勇隊同志会 慰霊祭 自由参加形式へ 会員の高齢化など要因|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-4-13|page=24}}</ref>。昭和47年（[[1972年]]）から元満州開拓義勇隊県同志会の主催により毎年行われてきたが会員の高齢化により継続が難しくなっていた<ref name="shimotsuke2"/>。翌年以降は毎年4月に自由参拝の形式で行われることになった<ref name="shimotsuke2"/>。
平成22年[[8月14日]]、大東亜戦争末期に爆弾を積んだ飛行機や[[人間魚雷]]「[[回天]]」などで敵艦突入を命じられ[[特別攻撃隊|特攻]]に出撃して戦死した20歳前後の特攻隊員たちを慰霊し語り継ごうと境内に顕彰碑「[[特攻勇士の像]]」が建立された<ref name="shimotsuke3">{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65年 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-8-14|page=3}}</ref>。
== 年中行事 == * 歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） * 初詣（[[1月1日]] - [[1月31日]]） * 祈年祭（[[2月17日]]） * 例祭（[[4月28日]]） * 戦没者追悼平和祈年祭（[[8月15日]]） * 七五三祭（[[11月15日]]前後） * 新嘗祭（[[11月23日]]）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『栃木県神社誌』、栃木県神社庁、2006年
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp 栃木県護国神社] （公式サイト） * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 栃木県護国神社（とちぎけんごこくじんじゃ）] （栃木県神社庁） * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}}
{{デフォルトソート:とちきけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:宇都宮市の歴史]] [[Category:宇都宮市の建築物]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[栃木県護国神社]] to [[Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 栃木県護国神社（栃木縣護國神社） |画像 = [[ファイル:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]陽西町1-37 |位置 = {{Coord|36|34|20|N|139|51|35|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=栃木県護国神社}} |祭神 = 戦没者 |社格 = 内務大臣指定護国神社 |創建 = [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]） |本殿 = 流造銅板葺 |別名 = |例祭 = [[4月28日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps を表示 |{{Location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|栃木県護国神社}}|position=left}} |栃木県地図を表示 }} }}
'''栃木県護国神社（栃木縣護國神社）'''（とちぎけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）であり、[[境内]]には護国会館がある。
[[明治維新]]、[[日清戦争]]、[[日露戦争]]、[[大東亜戦争]]などの[[戦没者]]や[[警察]]、[[消防]]、[[自衛隊]]の[[殉職]]者などの旧[[宇都宮藩]]、栃木県関係の戦没者55,361柱を祀る。
[[天皇]]・[[皇后]]が日本全国で唯一公式に[[親拝]]している護国神社で、[[世界平和]]と人類共存を祈る神社とされている。大東亜戦争後、[[海外]]各[[戦地]]の[[慰霊]][[巡拝]]を[[国籍]]や当時の敵味方の区別なく永年に亘って継続しており、[[皇族]]や[[神社本庁]]とも所縁が深く、天皇・皇后が親拝した時の写真が[[本殿]]正面左右上方に掲げられている。
なお、本殿手前には、[[船村徹]]が寄贈した[[大日本帝国海軍]]の[[零式艦上戦闘機]]の模型が展示されている。
== 歴史 == [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）、[[戊辰戦争]]に殉じた宇都宮藩[[藩主]][[戸田忠恕]]および[[藩士]]96柱の合計97柱を宇都宮市馬場町字表尾山に祀った'''宇都宮[[招魂社]]'''が起源である<ref name="#1">『栃木県神社誌』，221頁</ref>。以降、栃木県出身の戦没者、殉職[[警察官]]、殉職[[消防士]]らの殉死者を[[合祀]]している。[[昭和]]14年（[[1939年]]）、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]令により[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]指定護国神社となり、'''栃木県護国神社'''と改称し、翌年の昭和15年（[[1940年]]）には現在地に[[遷座]]し、殉職[[自衛官]]の殉死者も合祀している。
昭和22年（[[1947年]]）に'''彰徳神社'''と改称したが占領軍が去った昭和28年（[[1953年]]）に栃木県護国神社と改復称した<ref name="#1"/>。
[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）[[7月25日]]には[[上皇明仁|天皇]]のたっての希望により、天皇・[[上皇后美智子|皇后]]が境内敷地入口にある大鳥居から本殿までを自ら歩き、公式に親拝している。
平成22年（[[2010年]]）[[3月18日]]、シイの巨木の移植が行われた<ref name="shimotsuke1">{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-3-19|page=24}}</ref>。当社が昭和15年に現在地に[[遷座]]した際に境内南側の敷地に植えられたもので、[[栃木県道70号宇都宮今市線|県道70号線]]の拡張工事のために伐採される予定であったが、保存を望む声が高まり大鳥居の左側に移植されることになった<ref name="shimotsuke1"/>。
平成22年[[4月11日]]、第39回元満州開拓青年義勇隊慰霊祭が境内の慰霊碑前で行われた<ref name="shimotsuke2">{{cite news|title=宇都宮・元満州開拓義勇隊同志会 慰霊祭 自由参加形式へ 会員の高齢化など要因|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-4-13|page=24}}</ref>。昭和47年（[[1972年]]）から元満州開拓義勇隊県同志会の主催により毎年行われてきたが会員の高齢化により継続が難しくなっていた<ref name="shimotsuke2"/>。翌年以降は毎年4月に自由参拝の形式で行われることになった<ref name="shimotsuke2"/>。
平成22年[[8月14日]]、大東亜戦争末期に爆弾を積んだ飛行機や[[人間魚雷]]「[[回天]]」などで敵艦突入を命じられ[[特別攻撃隊|特攻]]に出撃して戦死した20歳前後の特攻隊員たちを慰霊し語り継ごうと境内に顕彰碑「[[特攻勇士の像]]」が建立された<ref name="shimotsuke3">{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65年 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-8-14|page=3}}</ref>。
== 年中行事 == * 歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） * 初詣（[[1月1日]] - [[1月31日]]） * 祈年祭（[[2月17日]]） * 例祭（[[4月28日]]） * 戦没者追悼平和祈年祭（[[8月15日]]） * 七五三祭（[[11月15日]]前後） * 新嘗祭（[[11月23日]]）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『栃木県神社誌』、栃木県神社庁、2006年
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp 栃木県護国神社] （公式サイト） * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 栃木県護国神社（とちぎけんごこくじんじゃ）] （栃木県神社庁） * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}}
{{デフォルトソート:とちきけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:宇都宮市の歴史]] [[Category:宇都宮市の建築物]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]]

{{afc comment|1=see [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.57222 | longitude = 139.85972 | deities = War dead, including 55,361 spirits from Tochigi Prefecture | founded = 1872 (Meiji 5) | structure = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = April 28 | map = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps display |{{location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Display Tochigi Prefecture map }} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[Boshin War]], [[First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Utsunomiya]] [[Category:War memorials in Japan]] [[Category:Meiji period establishments]] [[Category:Pacific War memorials]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|栃木県護国神社|version=99332893|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.57222 | longitude = 139.85972 | deities = War dead, including 55,361 spirits from Tochigi Prefecture | founded = 1872 (Meiji 5) | structure = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = April 28 | map = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps display |{{location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Display Tochigi Prefecture map }} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[Boshin War]], [[First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{citation needed|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|栃木県護国神社|version=99332893|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.57222 | longitude = 139.85972 | deities = War dead, including 55,361 spirits from Tochigi Prefecture | founded = 1872 (Meiji 5) | structure = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = April 28 | map = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps display |{{location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Display Tochigi Prefecture map }} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[Boshin War]], [[First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|栃木県護国神社|version=99332893|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.57222 | longitude = 139.85972 | deities = War dead, including 55,361 spirits from Tochigi Prefecture | founded = 1872 (Meiji 5) | structure = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = April 28 | map = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps display |{{location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Display Tochigi Prefecture map }} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}) located in {{ill|Utsunomiya, Tochigi|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[Boshin War]], [[First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadaaki Toda|qq=}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toru Funamura|qq=}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|栃木県護国神社|version=99332893|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]
