revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] | location = [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.57222 | longitude = 139.85972 | deities = War dead, including 55,361 spirits from Tochigi Prefecture | founded = 1872 (Meiji 5) | structure = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = April 28 | map = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps display |{{location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Display Tochigi Prefecture map }} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[Boshin War]], [[First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Toda Tadayuki|lt=Toda Tadayuki|lt=Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadaaki Toda|qq=}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toru Funamura|qq=}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|栃木県護国神社|version=99332893|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

{{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toru Funamura|qq=}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] | location = [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.57222 | longitude = 139.85972 | deities = War dead, including 55,361 spirits from Tochigi Prefecture | founded = 1872 (Meiji 5) | structure = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = April 28 | map = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps display |{{location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Display Tochigi Prefecture map }} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[Boshin War]], [[First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Toda Tadayuki|lt=Toda Tadayuki|lt=Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadaaki Toda|qq=}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toru Funamura|qq=}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|栃木県護国神社|version=99332893|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]] [[Category:宇都宮市の建築物]] [[Category:宇都宮市の歴史]]

news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toru Funamura|qq=}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ successful]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]] [[Category:宇都宮市の建築物]] [[Category:宇都宮市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] | location = [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.57222 | longitude = 139.85972 | deities = War dead, including 55,361 spirits from Tochigi Prefecture | founded = 1872 (Meiji 5) | structure = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = April 28 | map = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps display |{{location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Display Tochigi Prefecture map }} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[Boshin War]], [[First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Toda Tadayuki|lt=Toda Tadayuki|lt=Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadaaki Toda|qq=}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toru Funamura|qq=}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]] [[Category:宇都宮市の建築物]] [[Category:宇都宮市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]

They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[Boshin War]], [[First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Toda Tadayuki|lt=Toda Tadayuki|lt=Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadaaki Toda|qq=}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toru Funamura|qq=}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|栃木県護国神社|version=99332893|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]] [[Category:Buildings in Utsunomiya�]] [[Category:宇都宮市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Commons category|Tochigiken-Gokoku-jinja}}

It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] | location = [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.57222 | longitude = 139.85972 | deities = War dead, including 55,361 spirits from Tochigi Prefecture | founded = 1872 (Meiji 5) | structure = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = April 28 | map = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps display |{{location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Display Tochigi Prefecture map }} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[Boshin War]], [[First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Toda Tadayuki|lt=Toda Tadayuki|lt=Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadaaki Toda|qq=}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toru Funamura|qq=}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|栃木県護国神社|version=99332893|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]] [[Category:Buildings in Utsunomiya]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Commons category|Tochigiken-Gokoku-jinja}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] | location = [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.57222 | longitude = 139.85972 | deities = War dead, including 55,361 spirits from Tochigi Prefecture | founded = 1872 (Meiji 5) | structure = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = April 28 | map = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps display |{{location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Display Tochigi Prefecture map }} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Toda Tadayuki|lt=Toda Tadayuki|lt=Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadaaki Toda|qq=}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toru Funamura|qq=}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|栃木県護国神社|version=99332893|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]] [[Category:Buildings in Utsunomiya]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Commons category|Tochigiken-Gokoku-jinja}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] | location = [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.57222 | longitude = 139.85972 | deities = War dead, including 55,361 spirits from Tochigi Prefecture | founded = 1872 (Meiji 5) | structure = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = April 28 | map = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps display |{{location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Display Tochigi Prefecture map }} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Toda Tadayuki|lt=Toda Tadayuki|lt=Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadaaki Toda|qq=}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[:en:Home Ministry|Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toru Funamura|qq=}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|栃木県護国神社|version=99332893|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]] [[Category:Buildings in Utsunomiya]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Commons category|Tochigiken-Gokoku-jinja}}

In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[:en:Kamikaze|Kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toru Funamura|qq=}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|栃木県護国神社|version=99332893|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]] [[Category:Buildings in Utsunomiya]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Commons category|Tochigiken-Gokoku-jinja}}

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:うつのみや百景]] into [[Category:100 Views of Utsunomiya]] via bot
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Tochigi Gokoku-jinja 001.jpg|300px]] | location = [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 36.57222 | longitude = 139.85972 | deities = War dead, including 55,361 spirits from Tochigi Prefecture | founded = 1872 (Meiji 5) | structure = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = April 28 | map = {{Switcher |{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.565|frame-longitude=139.872}} |Wikimedia maps display |{{location map|Japan Tochigi|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Display Tochigi Prefecture map }} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[:en:Japan Self-Defense Forces|Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Toda Tadayuki|lt=Toda Tadayuki|lt=Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadaaki Toda|qq=}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[:en:Home Ministry|Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[:en:Kamikaze|Kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toru Funamura|qq=}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|栃木県護国神社|version=99332893|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:100 Views of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Utsunomiya]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Commons category|Tochigiken-Gokoku-jinja}}

They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} In 1996 before a visit the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] inquired about potential war criminals at the shrine. {{Citation |title=Yasukuni Fundamentalism |date=2021-07-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.2307/j.ctv1jpf1pk.11 |work= |pages=207 |access-date=2023-10-25 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press}} '''Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|栃木県護国神社|1=Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|Tochigi-ken Gokoku Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]]) located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It honors spirits from Tochigi Prefecture who died in wars such as the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[Russo-Japanese War]], and [[Pacific War]] as well as deceased police officers, firefighters, and members of the [[:en:Japan Self-Defense Forces|Japan Self-Defense Forces]]. == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1872 (Meiji 5) when 97 spirits from the Boshin War, including those of Lord {{ill|Toda Tadayuki|lt=Toda Tadayuki|lt=Tadaaki Toda|ja|戸田忠恕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadaaki Toda|qq=}}, the former [[daimyo]] of Utsunomiya, and his retainers, were enshrined in Utsunomiya's Baba Town at the site of the original {{nihongo|Utsunomiya Shōkonsha|宇都宮招魂社}}.栃木県神社誌, p. 221 In 1939 (Showa 14), the shrine was designated a [[:en:Home Ministry|Home Ministry]]-approved Gokoku Shrine and renamed Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. It was relocated to its current site in 1940 (Showa 15). After the war, it was briefly renamed {{nihongo|Shōtoku Shrine|彰徳神社}} in 1947, but reverted to its former name in 1953 following the withdrawal of occupation forces. On July 25, 1996 (Heisei 8), Emperor {{nihongo|Akihito|上皇明仁}} and Empress {{nihongo|Michiko|上皇后美智子}} officially visited and paid their respects at the shrine. The event marked the first instance of a Gokoku Shrine being officially visited by the Emperor and Empress. In 2010 (Heisei 22), a prominent Japanese evergreen oak tree within the shrine grounds was transplanted to make way for road expansion, demonstrating efforts to preserve the area's heritage.{{cite news|title=見守られて引っ越し 護国神社のシイの木 地域のシンボルへ期待|newspaper=[[下野新聞]]|date=2010-03-19}} The shrine also hosts memorials, including a monument dedicated in 2010 to young men who perished in [[:en:Kamikaze|Kamikaze]] missions during the [[Pacific War]].{{cite news|title=とちぎ戦後65th year 節目に「[[特攻勇士の像]]」 元将校ら「語り継ぎたい」 宇都宮の護国神社 あす除幕式|newspaper=下野新聞|date=2010-08-14}} == Annual Events == * '''New Year's Festival''' (January 1) * '''First Shrine Visit''' (January 1–31) * '''Kinen-sai''' (February 17) * '''Reitaisai''' (April 28) * '''War Memorial Peace Festival''' (August 15) * '''Shichi-Go-San Festival''' (November 15) * '''Niiname-sai''' (November 23) == Notable Features == The shrine grounds include a model of a [[:en:Mitsubishi_A6M_Zero|Mitsubishi A6M Zero]] fighter plane, donated by famed composer {{ill|Toru Funamura|ja|船村徹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toru Funamura|qq=}}, displayed near the main hall. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.gr.jp Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tochigi-jinjacho.or.jp/?p=594 Tochigi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Agency] * {{Twitter|tochigi_gokoku}} * {{Facebook|137284933107090}} }} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|栃木県護国神社|version=99332893|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:ochigi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:栃木県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Tochigi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:100 Views of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Utsunomiya]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Commons category|Tochigiken-Gokoku-jinja}}
